reasons: Gatotkaca can force enemies into a crowd-control window, giving Pharsa a safer long-range burst opportunity.
Pharsa can punish the area Gatotkaca controls without standing inside the first engage.

proof.summary: Gatotkaca creates the engage and grouping window, while Pharsa follows with ranged magic damage into the controlled area.
worksBestWhen: Gatotkaca engages after Pharsa is ready and in follow-up range.
Pharsa saves her main burst for Gatotkaca's knock-up or taunt window.
failureCases: Gatotkaca dives too far away from Pharsa.
Pharsa is forced to reposition instead of casting.

reasons: Gatotkaca can start the fight and hold enemy attention, giving Odette a better window to cast AoE damage.
Odette benefits when Gatotkaca forces enemies to stay near the teamfight center.

proof.summary: Gatotkaca starts or stabilizes the teamfight, while Odette converts the controlled area into sustained AoE magic pressure.
worksBestWhen: Gatotkaca lands on multiple enemies or forces a tight fight area.
Odette casts after enemies are already slowed, taunted, or knocked airborne.
failureCases: Odette is interrupted before her damage finishes.
Gatotkaca catches only a low-value target.
The enemy saves displacement or suppression for Odette.

reasons: Gatotkaca can create frontline space that helps Irithel keep moving and attacking from behind him.
His taunt and knock-up can punish enemies that try to dive Irithel's damage position.

proof.summary: Gatotkaca disrupts enemy engage paths, allowing Irithel to maintain mobile basic attacks from safer spacing.
worksBestWhen: Gatotkaca peels near Irithel instead of diving too far away.
Irithel plays around Gatotkaca's frontline zone.
failureCases: Irithel is caught before she can kite.

reasons: Gatotkaca can force a teamfight area where Luo Yi can add follow-up control and displacement pressure.
Luo Yi can extend Gatotkaca's engage value when enemies are already grouped or forced into narrow paths.

proof.summary: Gatotkaca starts the fight, while Luo Yi adds a second layer of control and positioning pressure around the same fight area.
worksBestWhen: Luo Yi prepares positioning before Gatotkaca commits.
The fight happens in a narrow area where enemies cannot easily spread.
failureCases: Luo Yi is too far to follow the engage.
Gatotkaca and Luo Yi pressure different targets.
Enemy mobility resets before the follow-up control lands.

reasons: Gatotkaca can absorb the first fight layer so Claude has a safer timing to enter with AoE damage.
Claude can follow Gatotkaca's disruption once enemy control and attention are drawn forward.

proof.summary: Gatotkaca controls or distracts the enemy formation, while Claude follows with AoE damage after the engage starts.
worksBestWhen: Claude waits for Gatotkaca to force key crowd control first.
Gatotkaca disrupts the enemy formation long enough for Claude to enter.
failureCases: Claude enters before Gatotkaca creates space.
Enemy burst is saved for Claude.
Gatotkaca misses the main damage threats.
